content.problemStatement:
You have a **simple weighted complete undirected** graph on a vertex set with NNN vertices, numbered 111 to NNN. The weights follow a special property as described below.

You are given an array AAA of size NNN (1‚Č§Ai‚Č§201 \le A_i \le 201‚Č§Ai‚Äč‚Č§20), and the weight of the edge joining vertices XXX and YYY can be described as follows:


If AX=AYA_X = A_YAX‚Äč=AY‚Äč, the weight is ‚ą£X‚ąíY‚ą£|X - Y|‚ą£X‚ąíY‚ą£
If AX‚Č†AYA_X \ne A_YAX‚ÄčÓÄ†=AY‚Äč, the weight is 2‚čÖ‚ą£X‚ąíY‚ą£2 \cdot |X - Y|2‚čÖ‚ą£X‚ąíY‚ą£
You are given QQQ queries, each containing 222 nodes XXX and YYY, and you must print the distance between vertex XXX and vertex YYY. The distance is defined as the smallest sum of weights on a path from XXX to YYY.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line contains 222 integers each - NNN and QQQ, the number of vertices and the number of queries.
The second line contains NNN integers - A1,A2,‚Ä¶,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1‚Äč,A2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äč.
The next QQQ lines contain 222 integers each - XXX and YYY, representing a query.




Output Format
For each test case, output QQQ integers on a single line, the answers to the queries in order.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 4‚čÖ1044 \cdot 10^44‚čÖ104
The sum of QQQ over all test cases does not exceed 2‚čÖ1062 \cdot 10^62‚čÖ106

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test Case 1 : Here are the explanations for 222 of the queries:


Query 1 : We can take the path 6‚Üí5‚Üí4‚Üí16 \rightarrow 5 \rightarrow 4 \rightarrow 16‚Üí5‚Üí4‚Üí1. The edges (in order) have weights of 1+2+3=61 + 2 + 3 = 61+2+3=6.


Query 5 : We can take the path 3‚Üí4‚Üí53 \rightarrow 4 \rightarrow 53‚Üí4‚Üí5, which have weights 2+2=42 + 2 = 42+2=4.
